title: Coding the Backpropagation Pass
concept: Calculating gradients of loss with respect to weights using the chain rule and updating weights.

exercises.prompt: What is the purpose of the Cross-Entropy loss function in training a model?

options.text: To calculate the accuracy of the model's predictions.
To measure how far off the model's predictions are from the actual labels.
To update the weights of the model directly.

exercises.explain: The Cross-Entropy loss function helps us understand how well our model's predictions match the real labels.

cards.title: The Backpropagation Pass
cards.body: In this lesson, we will learn how to calculate gradients of loss and update weights to improve our model.

cards.title: What is Backpropagation?
cards.body: Backpropagation is a method used in training neural networks, where we calculate gradients to efficiently update model parameters.

cards.title: Learning from Mistakes
cards.body: Think of backpropagation like a student reviewing their incorrect answers on a test to learn how to improve in the future.

cards.title: Calculating Gradients
cards.body: To find how to change our weights, we calculate how much the loss changes with small changes in the weights.

cards.title: Myth: Weights are random
cards.body: Some people believe that weights are randomly chosen each time. In reality, we learn and adjust them through backpropagation!

cards.title: Gradient Descent Visualization
cards.body: The gradient indicates the direction to update weights for decreasing loss.

right.text: Updates weights the right way to minimize loss.

exercises.prompt: What does the gradient tell us in backpropagation?

options.text: The direction to change weights to reduce loss.
The final accuracy of the model.
The cost of running the model.

exercises.explain: The gradient shows us how to adjust weights to make the loss lower, guiding our updates.

exercises.prompt: True or False: Backpropagation only works with linear models.
exercises.explain: Backpropagation is effective for various types of models, including non-linear ones like neural networks.

exercises.prompt: To update the model's weights, we use the __________ of loss regarding the weights.
exercises.sentence: To update the model's weights, we use the gradients of loss regarding the weights.
exercises.explain: The correct answer is 'gradients', as we calculate these to know how to adjust the weights.

exercises.prompt: Which statement correctly describes the purpose of the chain rule in backpropagation?

options.text: It helps us train larger models faster.
It allows us to compute gradients for each weight to update them.
It randomly increases the weights each step.

exercises.explain: The chain rule enables us to break down the gradient calculations, ensuring we can effectively update each weight.

exercises.prompt: During backpropagation, why do we calculate how loss changes with respect to weights?

options.text: To determine how to improve our model by updating weights.
To calculate the model's performance on training data.

exercises.explain: Calculating the loss with respect to weights helps guide updates toward reducing error, leading to better model performance.
